title: message_set reactions: reaction_id and emoji values are stored without length or character validation
description: jmap.rs message_set update, reaction path: reaction_id comes from the JMAP patch path (e.g. 'reactions/someId'), and emoji comes from the patch object's 'emoji' field. Neither has a length cap or character-set check. store.rs set_message_reaction writes reaction_id as a JSON map key and emoji as a JSON string value into the reactions column. An authenticated client can store a reaction_id of arbitrary length (the reactions JSON object key), and an emoji of arbitrary length. The reactions column is fetched and re-serialized on every reaction operation, so a large reactions blob grows per mutation. Scenario: client sends Message/set with reactions/<100KB string>: {emoji: <100KB string>} — stored in DB, returned in every Message/get.
